questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A sole proprietorship is the simplest and most common form of business organization.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has the least formalities and least regulatory compliance.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason for a sole proprietorship being the simplest form is indeed due to its minimal formalities and regulatory compliance.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Partnership has a perpetual existence.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It acquires a legal entity separate from its partners.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (D) is correct because a partnership does not acquire a legal entity separate from its partners, hence, it does not have perpetual existence. Its existence is dependent on the partners.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A company is a separate legal entity from its shareholders.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It acquires a legal entity separate from its owners.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason a company is a separate legal entity from its shareholders is indeed because it acquires a legal entity separate from its owners.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A partnership is a type of private limited company.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has limited liability for its partners.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (C) is correct because a partnership is not a type of private limited company, though it does have limited liability for its partners.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A sole proprietorship has no separate legal existence.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It is not registered with the Registrar of Companies.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason a sole proprietorship has no separate legal existence is indeed because it is not registered with the Registrar of Companies.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): Partnership does not have limited liability for its partners.
questions.reason: Reason (R): Each partner has unlimited personal liability.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason a partnership does not have limited liability for its partners is indeed because each partner has unlimited personal liability.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A private company is a type of public limited company.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has a minimum paid-up capital of Rs. 5 lakhs.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (D) is correct because a private company is not a type of public limited company, though it does have a minimum paid-up capital of Rs. 5 lakhs.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A company has a perpetual succession.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It can be sued or sue in its own name.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason a company has perpetual succession is indeed because it can be sued or sue in its own name.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A sole proprietorship is a type of partnership.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It has limited liability for its owner.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (D) is correct because a sole proprietorship is not a type of partnership, though it does have limited liability for its owner.

questions.assertion: Assertion (A): A company has no ownership restrictions.
questions.reason: Reason (R): It can be owned by any individual, institution, or government.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Option (A) is correct because the reason a company has no ownership restrictions is indeed because it can be owned by any individual, institution, or government.
